plan.title: Add epic-staleness and stranded-slice lifecycle nudges with trichotomy

narratives.Description: Adds detection for stale undecomposed epics and stranded partially-completed epics, surfacing a finish, cancel-and-remove, or accept-as-tech-debt trichotomy when an epic is dormant beyond the configured threshold. The nudges feed the shared session-start ranking so they appear within the budgeted welcome line.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: 1. Add a stranded-slice and epic-staleness detector module under scripts/ that reads epic and child status from the lifecycle folders and computes dormancy against epicStrandedDays and epicStalenessDays. 2. Wire the detector into scripts/triage_welcome.py and add tests in tests/cli/test_triage_welcome.py asserting the trichotomy nudge fires for a dormant partially-completed epic.
narratives.UserStory: As a maintainer, I want stranded epics flagged with clear options, so that partially-built features do not silently accrue debt.

items.title: Given an epic dormant beyond epicStrandedDays with a completed child, when triage:welcome runs, then it emits the finish-cancel-accept trichotomy.

narrative.Acceptance: Given an epic dormant beyond epicStrandedDays with a completed child, when triage:welcome runs, then it emits the finish-cancel-accept trichotomy.

items.title: Given an undecomposed epic older than epicStalenessDays, when triage:welcome runs, then it emits a needs-estimation nudge.

narrative.Acceptance: Given an undecomposed epic older than epicStalenessDays, when triage:welcome runs, then it emits a needs-estimation nudge.

items.title: When a stranded epic is accepted as debt, then the detector records a tech-debt follow-up reference and stops re-nudging.

narrative.Acceptance: When a stranded epic is accepted as debt, then the detector records a tech-debt follow-up reference and stops re-nudging.

expected_outputs: triage:welcome emits stranded-slice trichotomy and epic-staleness nudges
tests prove the dormant partially-completed epic path fires

references.title: Issue #1419: RFC: Agentic Prioritization & Coordination Model for Deft Directive
